name: Retry with goto example
description: Demonstrates the retry-from-earlier recovery pattern using last_step_status + condition.thenStep + a goto step.
goal: Navigate to a site, detect a failure via the runner's step-outcome variables, run a recovery handler, and jump back to the original step to try again.

steps.name: Open target page
steps.description: This step is intentionally allowed to fail so the condition + goto recovery pattern can fire. In a real automation this would be a login, an MFA prompt, or any step whose failure should trigger a recovery flow rather than an abort.

steps.name: Did step-open fail?
steps.description: Reads last_step_status via variable_equals. If step-open failed, jump to the recovery handler; otherwise jump past it to the normal continuation.

steps.name: Recovery handler
steps.description: Run whatever is needed to prepare for a retry — clear cookies, close a modal, switch browser profiles, re-request an OTP, etc. This example just issues a short delay to simulate the recovery work.

steps.name: Jump back to step-open and retry
steps.description: Unconditionally resets the runner's instruction pointer to step-open. The execution cap (1000 total step executions per run) catches the infinite-loop case if step-open never recovers, so broken automations fail fast with a clear error instead of hanging.

steps.name: Normal continuation
steps.description: This step only runs when the condition's elseStep fires — i.e. when step-open succeeded. On the first successful pass, the automation completes here.
